texto: The English may love gardening and fishing, but they have never struck me as being close to nature. Their way of expression is 'the hollyhocks are awfully good' sort of thing, all done in very good taste. The savagery of nature is something they don't dwell upon.
explicacao: Citação de Denholm Elliott sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “The English may love gardening and fishing, but they have never struck me as being close to natur…” articula amor, love, nature em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como nature e good.

frase_original: The English may love gardening and fishing, but they have never struck me as being close to nature. Their way of expression is 'the hollyhocks are awfully good' sort of thing, all done in very good taste. The savagery of nature is something they don't dwell upon.
